description: Fail-loud: chronon3d_cli render must abort loudly when a video asset is structurally corrupt (e.g., truncated MP4, invalid codec). Canonical error vocabulary: Composition/Layer/Asset/Path tokens in stderr + video-decode context, NO silent fallback markers, exit code != 0, output MP4 absent.
_video_corruption_setup_hint: Pre-create /tmp/chronon_corrupt_video_harness/corrupt.mp4 with a deliberately truncated MP4 header (e.g., zero-byte file) before invoking the CLI. The harness directory is supplied; the rotting byte stream is provided externally to keep the JSON declarative.
honesty_note: Per AGENTS.md §honesty: the corroption-byte-stream harness is EXTERNAL to this JSON (because binary fixtures do not belong in tracked JSON). The harness directory is created in the bash script's setup phase if absent; the CLI invocation may then fall back to AssetResolver-failure if the harness is unprepared — the assertion is the SAME (fail-loud, not the specific rot trigger).
